text:
Hospital classification as cost centers and the use of mutual allocation methods reduce distortions arising from cost allocation. However, implementing mutual allocation is impractical due to the complexity of the operations involved in matrix algebra. This paper aims to propose a methodology for implementing reciprocal cost allocation in an algebraic formulation free of matrix operations.

Using a case study of a municipal hospital, the study demonstrated the operationalization of the reciprocal method using the standard Excel spreadsheet and the specially designed software included in the hospital's management system. This approach retains the basic principles of matrix calculus but at the same time translates them into a simpler, more transparent and more accurate calculation process. The developed system was implemented using the Delphi 7 compiler with Object Pascal programming language and Firebird 2.1 database manager. It can be easily used in any healthcare institution in order to optimize the distribution of indirect costs and thus to achieve a higher accuracy in making the relevant economic decisions.
